channel.title: Comparator, OpAmp & Voltage Reference on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink
channel.description: Recent content in Comparator, OpAmp & Voltage Reference on MPLAB Device Blocks for Simulink

item.title: MCHP High-Speed Analog Comparator Documentation
item.description:
Fast threshold detection and PWM integration for motor control and power applications

When to use:

When to use:

  • dsPIC33E/C target (dsPIC33EP, dsPIC33EV, dsPIC33CK, dsPIC33CH families)
  • Fast overcurrent/overvoltage protection for motor control (sub-microsecond response)
  • Direct PWM fault integration needed (hardware-based, no CPU latency)
  • Programmable threshold via internal DAC (eliminate external voltage divider)
  • Multiple comparators available (up to 4 instances per device)

When NOT to use:

  • Legacy dsPIC30F/33FJ target — use Comparator/Op-Amp/Volt Ref block instead
  • SAM target device — use ACC SAMx block instead
  • Slope compensation needed for peak current mode — use HS Comparator w/ Slope block
  • ADC precision required — comparator provides only binary HIGH/LOW output

Overview

The MCHP High-Speed Analog Comparator block configures the on-chip analog comparators for fast threshold detection applications. These comparators provide critical real-time monitoring for motor control overcurrent protection, power supply fault detection, and zero-crossing detection. With response times in the nanosecond range, they can directly trigger PWM fault events without CPU intervention.

item.title: MCHP High-Speed Analog Comparator with Slope Compensation Documentation
item.description:
Advanced peak current mode control with programmable slope compensation

When to use:

When to use:

  • Peak current mode control for DC-DC converters (buck, boost, flyback)
  • Duty cycle >50% — slope compensation prevents sub-harmonic oscillation
  • Integrated ramp generation needed (automatic synchronization with PWM)
  • Variable slope adjustment required for optimal stability margins
  • Multiple comparators with individual slope compensation (up to 8 instances)

When NOT to use:

item.title: MCHP Op-Amp Documentation
item.description:
Integrated operational amplifiers for signal conditioning and analog processing

When to use:

When to use:

  • dsPIC33C/CH/CK target device (on-chip op-amp peripheral available)
  • Signal conditioning needed (sensor buffering, gain stages, filtering)
  • Eliminating external op-amp chips — reduce BOM cost and board space
  • Direct connection to ADC for optimized signal chain
  • Voltage follower (buffer), non-inverting, inverting, or differential amplifier

When NOT to use:

  • Older dsPIC30F/33F/33E — no dedicated op-amp peripheral (use external chip)
  • Programmable gain required — use PGA block instead (digitally controlled)
  • Rail-to-rail output required — check device specs for output swing
  • High bandwidth >1 MHz — internal op-amp may not meet speed requirements

Overview

The MCHP_OP_AMP block provides access to the integrated operational amplifiers available in dsPIC33C, dsPIC33CH, and dsPIC33CK microcontrollers. These on-chip op-amps eliminate the need for external amplification circuitry in many applications, reducing BOM cost and board space while improving signal integrity.

item.title: MCHP PGA Documentation
item.description:
Digitally-controlled gain amplifier with calibration for precision analog signal conditioning

When to use:

When to use:

  • dsPIC33C/CH/CK target device (on-chip PGA peripheral available)
  • Digitally-controlled gain needed (1×, 2×, 4×, 8×, 16×, 32× software-selectable)
  • Runtime gain adjustment for automatic ranging or adaptive signal conditioning
  • Precision sensor interfaces with calibration (offset/gain correction registers)
  • Differential input mode for common-mode noise rejection

When NOT to use:

  • Older dsPIC30F/33F/33E — no PGA peripheral available (use external PGA chip)
  • Fixed gain suffices — use Op-Amp block instead (no need for programmable gain)
  • Gain outside 1×-32× range — PGA limited to these discrete values
  • Ultra-low noise required — external instrumentation amplifier may be better

Overview

The MCHP_PGA block provides access to the Programmable Gain Amplifier (PGA) peripheral available in select dsPIC33C, dsPIC33CH, and dsPIC33CK devices. The PGA offers digitally-controlled gain settings with calibration capabilities, enabling precision signal conditioning for sensor inputs and ADC front-end applications.

item.title: MCHP Comparator / Op-Amp / Voltage Reference
item.description:
Legacy comparator, op-amp, and voltage reference configuration for dsPIC30F and dsPIC33FJ devices

When to use:

When to use:

  • Legacy dsPIC30F or dsPIC33FJ target device (DS70357 & DS70648 datasheets)
  • Older project maintenance or existing design migration
  • Internal CVR voltage reference needed (16-level programmable reference)
  • Op-amp functionality available on device (unity gain, non-inverting, inverting)
  • Comparator with digital filtering or blanking logic required

When NOT to use:
